title: Module 0: Setup Your Portfolio
description: Foundation module. Set up your personal AI training portfolio and create your first agent.

modules.title: Context / Theory
modules.description: The right mental model for working with AI.

steps.title: What AI Actually Is (No Jargon)
steps.content: AI — artificial intelligence — is software that can understand language, answer questions, and generate content. The AI tools you'll use in this course are conversational: you type something, and they respond. That's it. No coding, no configuration, no special syntax. Just type what you need, the same way you'd message a colleague.

steps.title: The Mental Model: AI as a Capable Assistant
steps.content:
The best way to think about AI is as a very capable assistant who needs direction. It's not going to replace you — it doesn't know your company, your context, your judgment. But it can do a lot of the heavy lifting: research, writing, analysis, formatting, brainstorming, checking work, and building things.

Your job is to direct it. AI's job is to do the work — fast.

steps.title: The 80/20 Rule
steps.content:
AI gets you 80% of the way there — fast. Your expertise finishes the job.

This is the pattern you'll see over and over throughout this course:
- AI drafts, you refine
- AI researches, you verify
- AI builds, you review

The combination is what makes it powerful.

steps.title: What AI Is Good At vs. What It's Bad At
steps.content:
**AI is good at:**
- Explaining concepts clearly at whatever level you need
- Drafting written content (emails, memos, reports, summaries)
- Analyzing data, documents, and spreadsheets
- Following instructions and repeating processes consistently
- Brainstorming ideas and alternatives
- Building simple tools, templates, and automations

**AI is not good at:**
- Knowing your company's specific context (unless you tell it)
- Making judgment calls that require human experience
- Guaranteeing 100% accuracy on specific facts or numbers
- Replacing the need for professional expertise on critical matters
- Accessing your internal systems (unless connected via tools)

steps.title: How to Think About Prompting
steps.content:
"Prompting" sounds technical, but it's just clear communication. When you give AI a prompt, you're telling it what you want — like giving instructions to a colleague. The clearer your instructions, the better the result.

You don't need special formulas or templates. Just be specific.

modules.title: Now To Do
modules.description: Hands-on steps. Set up your accounts, create your training portfolio organization, and build your first agent.

steps.title: Step 1: Create Your Free Accounts
steps.content: Create free accounts on ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, and My AI For One.

steps.title: Step 2: Create Your Training Portfolio Organization
steps.content: Go to My AI For One and create a new organization called My Training Portfolio.

steps.title: Step 3: Create Your Using AI Series Agent
steps.content: Create your first agent — the Using AI Series Agent — with portfolio tracking instructions.

steps.title: Step 4: Test Your Agent
steps.content: Open a conversation with your new Using AI Series Agent and send it a quick message.

steps.title: Step 5: Quick Test of an AI Platform
steps.content: Test an AI platform with a simple question.

modules.title: Application
modules.description: How the setup and mental model from this module apply every time you start something new with AI.

steps.title: Starting a New Project at Work
steps.content: Before diving into any new work project, think about what AI can help with and set up the right tools.

steps.title: Onboarding a New Team Member
steps.content: Share the mental model from this module — AI as an assistant that needs direction.

steps.title: Evaluating AI Tools
steps.content: You'll have a practical framework for assessing AI tools.

steps.title: Building an AI Practice
steps.content: Your training portfolio is the beginning of your personal AI practice.

steps.title: No Bonus for Module 0 — Just Get Started
steps.content: Module 0 is your foundation. Move on to Module 1.

modules.title: AI Terms
modules.description: Key terms introduced in this module.

steps.title: AI (Artificial Intelligence)
steps.content: Software that can understand language, answer questions, and generate content.

steps.title: LLM (Large Language Model)
steps.content: The technology behind tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Grok.

steps.title: Prompt
steps.content: The question or instruction you type into an AI tool.

steps.title: Agent
steps.content: A reusable AI setup where you've given the AI specific instructions about its role and expertise.

steps.title: Organization
steps.content: A workspace in My AI For One that groups your agents and projects together.

steps.title: Portfolio
steps.content: The collection of agents, tools, and artifacts you build throughout this course.
